description: Fermi estimates expressed as single words combined into triples, plus formulas, sources and values, following the same schema as the meteorology base data. Contains the disinformation-dam expected-value estimate discussed in the categorical-imperative working paper, and a meteorology-native worked example (raindrops in a thunderstorm).

words.name: Fermi
words.description: Enrico Fermi, Italian-American physicist after whom order-of-magnitude estimation is named

words.name: estimate
words.description: rough approximation of a quantity made without exact measurement

words.name: disinformation
words.description: false information spread deliberately to deceive

words.name: dam
words.description: barrier built to stop or restrict the flow of water or other material; here used metaphorically for a regulatory barrier against disinformation

words.name: expected
words.description: anticipated; in probability theory the probability-weighted average outcome

words.name: value
words.description: quantitative worth, magnitude or payoff assigned to a quantity

words.name: potential
words.description: latent or possible, as opposed to actual or realised

words.name: harm
words.description: damage or detrimental effect, including economic loss

words.name: democratic
words.description: relating to democracy as a system of government

words.name: decline
words.description: sustained decrease or deterioration over time

words.name: probability
words.description: numerical measure between 0 and 1 of how likely an event is

words.name: causal
words.description: relating to or expressing a cause-and-effect relationship

words.name: link
words.description: connection between two entities

words.name: leverage
words.description: ratio of effect to effort; here the fraction of potential harm a feasible measure could avert

words.name: measure
words.description: intervention or action taken; also the numerical result of a measurement

words.name: cost
words.description: resources consumed or value sacrificed to obtain something

words.name: test
words.description: controlled trial or experiment to check a hypothesis

words.name: decision
words.description: choice made between alternatives

words.name: uncertainty
words.description: state of limited knowledge about outcomes or values

words.name: raindrop
words.description: single drop of liquid water falling from a cloud

words.name: thunderstorm
words.description: convective storm producing thunder and lightning, typically with heavy rain

words.name: storm
words.description: violent atmospheric disturbance

words.name: cell
words.description: localised unit; here a single convective updraft column of a thunderstorm

words.name: area
words.description: two-dimensional extent of a surface

words.name: rain
words.description: liquid water droplets falling from clouds

words.name: rainfall
words.description: precipitation of rain accumulated over a given area and time

words.name: depth
words.description: vertical distance measured from a top surface downward

words.name: volume
words.description: three-dimensional space occupied by a body or quantity

words.name: total
words.description: sum or aggregate of parts

words.name: conformity
words.description: tendency of individuals to align beliefs or behaviour with a group

words.name: rate
words.description: frequency or proportion measured per unit of comparison

words.name: baseline
words.description: reference value used as a starting point for comparison

words.name: error
words.description: deviation from a correct or true value

words.name: machine
words.description: mechanical or computational device

words.name: influence
words.description: effect one entity has on another's state or behaviour

words.name: unconscious
words.description: below the level of conscious awareness or deliberate intent

words.name: takeover
words.description: act of assuming control of something

words.name: fact
words.description: statement consistent with observable reality

words.name: consensus
words.description: shared agreement among a group

words.name: gatekeeper
words.description: person or process controlling access to or flow through a channel

words.name: filter
words.description: process that selectively allows certain items to pass

words.name: social
words.description: relating to society or interaction among people

words.name: offloading
words.description: transferring a task or responsibility to another entity

triples.name: Fermi estimate
triples.description: order-of-magnitude estimation that decomposes an unknown quantity into a product of roughly knowable factors

triples.name: raindrop volume
triples.description: volume of a single representative raindrop

triples.name: leverage of the measure
triples.description: fraction of the potential harm that a feasible, rule-of-law-bound regulatory dam could plausibly avert

triples.name: cost of the test
triples.description: order-of-magnitude cost and risk of running the regulatory experiment, including risk to legitimate free expression

triples.name: decision under uncertainty
triples.description: choosing an action when outcomes and their probabilities are only roughly known

triples.name: raindrops in a thunderstorm
triples.description: order-of-magnitude number of raindrops falling from a single mid-size thunderstorm cell over its lifetime

triples.name: probability of the causal link
triples.description: subjective probability that digital sensory overload at least partly causes the observed cognitive and democratic decline

triples.name: storm cell area
triples.description: ground area covered by a single mid-size thunderstorm cell

triples.name: total rain volume
triples.description: volume of water deposited by the storm, the area times the rainfall depth

triples.name: total rainfall depth
triples.description: depth of water deposited by the storm over its lifetime

triples.name: potential harm from democratic decline
triples.description: monetised order-of-magnitude harm if the digital-overstimulation-to-democratic-decline link is real, dominated by populism-induced economic loss across affected economies

triples.name: disinformation dam expected value
triples.description: expected avoided harm of building a regulatory dam against deliberate flooding of public discourse with disinformation, used to decide whether testing the measure is worthwhile

triples.name: expected value to cost ratio
triples.description: ratio of expected avoided harm to test cost; a value far above one indicates the test is worthwhile

triples.name: value cost ratio in dam value
conformity rate
triples.description: percentage of individuals conforming to incorrect group opinions under social pressure, based on Berns et al. (2005)

triples.name: baseline error
triples.description: inherent human error rate when performing tasks in isolation without external influence

triples.name: machine influence
triples.description: error rate of individuals when presented with incorrect information from a computer source

triples.name: unconscious takeover
triples.description: share of participants who unconsciously adopt group distortions as their own reality

triples.name: fact consensus
triples.description: historical estimated reach of shared factual consensus under regulated 1970s media environments

triples.name: gatekeeper filter
triples.description: structural reduction of unverified disinformation throughput by media gatekeepers

formulas.name: disinformation dam expected value formula
formulas.expression: "disinformation dam expected value" = "potential harm from democratic decline" * "probability of the causal link" * "leverage of the measure"

formulas.name: expected value to cost ratio formula
formulas.expression: "expected value to cost ratio" = "disinformation dam expected value" / "cost of the test"

formulas.name: total rain volume formula
formulas.expression: "total rain volume" = "storm cell area" * "total rainfall depth"

formulas.name: raindrops in a thunderstorm formula
formulas.expression: "raindrops in a thunderstorm" = "total rain volume" / "raindrop volume"

sources.name: Fermi estimate
sources.description: an order-of-magnitude figure assumed for a Fermi estimation, deliberately rough and chosen to be defensible within a factor of a few

sources.name: by definition
sources.description: a value that is fixed by an unambiguous definition or convention rather than measured

sources.name: Berns et al. (2005)
sources.description: Berns GS, Chappelow J, Zink CF, Pagnoni G, Martin-Skurski ME, Richards J (2005): Neurobiological Correlates of Social Conformity and Independence During Mental Rotation. Biol Psychiatry 58:245-253

sources.name: Historical media estimation
sources.description: order-of-magnitude estimate of the share of facts on which the population reached a common consensus in the pre-internet broadcast media era

sources.name: Pre-internet virality estimation
sources.description: order-of-magnitude estimate of the share of fringe content that bypassed editorial gatekeepers and reached a mass audience before the rise of algorithmic social media
